entities.name: INTEGRATED NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: An approach to managing soil fertility and nutrient supply for sustainable agricultural production

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where the study is focused, specifically Sub-Saharan Africa

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing crops and livestock on suitable land

entities.name: ARID AND SEMIARID LANDS
entities.description: Lands characterized by low rainfall, comprising 34 percent of potentially suitable agricultural land in Africa

entities.name: RAINFED AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Farming that relies solely on rainfall for water, which is not viable in arid and semiarid lands

entities.name: SEMIARID REGIONS
entities.description: Areas with a shortened length of growing period between 75 and 129 days

entities.name: SUBHUMID ZONES
entities.description: Areas with a growing period between 180 and 269 days

entities.name: HUMID ZONES
entities.description: Areas with a growing period exceeding 270 days

entities.name: LENGTH OF GROWING PERIOD
entities.description: The duration of time during which plants can grow, varying by climatic zone

entities.name: AGROECOSYSTEMS
entities.description: The biological community of interacting organisms and their environment in an agricultural setting

entities.name: FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The set of practices used to manage agricultural production, which have evolved over time

entities.name: TRADITIONAL SHIFTING FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: A farming method involving the rotation of plots of land

entities.name: PERMANENT AND INTENSIFIED ARABLE AND MIXED FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Farming systems that are fixed in location and utilize intensive inputs

entities.name: COPING STRATEGIES
entities.description: Methods used by farmers to respond to environmental and socioeconomic changes

entities.name: BIOPHYSICAL AND SOCIOECONOMIC CIRCUMSTANCES
entities.description: The environmental and social factors that influence farming system changes

relationships.description: Studied and applied within the context of Sub-Saharan Africa
Location where agricultural production is potentially suitable
Too dry to support rainfed agriculture
Characterized by a shortened length of growing period
Characterized by a medium length of growing period
Characterized by a long length of growing period
Dynamics of agroecosystems are reflected in the changes of farming systems
Includes traditional shifting farming systems as a historical practice

relationships.target: PERMANENT AND INTENSIFIED ARABLE AND MIXED FARMING SYSTEMS
relationships.description: Includes permanent and intensified arable and mixed farming systems as a modern practice
Changes in farming systems serve as coping strategies
Used to respond to changing biophysical and socioeconomic circumstances
